description: Task Priority System - first evolving PRD slice

finalSuccessCriteria.description: Users can assign, view, edit, and filter task priority across the app, with priority persisted between sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Tasks persist a priority value of high, medium, or low
Users can see priority on task cards without opening details
Users can edit priority from the task edit flow
Users can filter the task list by priority
Relevant UI flows are verified in browser

planning.currentObjective: Display storyPriority in CLI story listings so users can see priority at a glance

prdChain.objective: Persist task priority in the data model
prdChain.notes: US-001 passed: storyPriority enum field added to schema and validated.

prdChain.objective: Show priority label when the CLI lists user stories

userStories.title: Display priority on story listings
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the storyPriority label beside each user story when the CLI lists or validates stories, so I can identify priority without opening details.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `node dist/index.js validate` (or the CLI list command) prints the storyPriority value (high/medium/low) alongside each story's id and title
Output visually distinguishes high from medium/low (e.g. prefix tag [HIGH], [MEDIUM], [LOW])
Typecheck passes (`npm run typecheck` exits 0 in cli/)
